attributes.action: Charge a fee to vehicles commuting through Richmond Park
attributes.additional_details: Richmond Park is one of the largest and most natural spaces in London and has become increasingly congested with vehicles who use it as a through road during their commute. This traffic makes the park feel increasingly less safe and accessible for recreational use while also causing significant pollution. I believe a toll fee would encourage considerate usage but this should exclude people parking there to enjoy the park itself.
attributes.background: There is an increasing problem of commuting traffic through the park and a small toll or fee would help to ease congestion, improve safety and encourage considerate usage. This would exclude vehicles who are parking there to visit the park.

rejection.details:
We can’t accept petitions that call for action at a local level.

You could ask your MP to take action on your concerns about this issue.

You can find contact details for your local MP here: https://members.parliament.uk/FindYourMP

You could also raise your issue with a local councillor who represents you. You can use this page to find out who your local councillors are, and how to contact them: https://www.gov.uk/find-your-local-councillors
